am: Morning. You don't have to do it all today — just the next small thing.
New day, low stakes. Start gentle.
Coffee first. Plans later. You've got time.
You woke up and you're trying. That's the whole job today.
Whatever you half-finished yesterday is still allowed to be half-finished. Begin anyway.
Today doesn't need to be big. It just needs to be yours.
Drink some water, unclench your jaw, and go be a person.
Small steps still count as walking.
You're allowed to start before you feel ready.
Be a little kinder to yourself than you think you deserve. It usually balances out.
pm: Whatever today was, it's allowed to just be over now. Rest easy.
You did enough. Even if it didn't feel like it.
Put it down. It'll still be there tomorrow, and so will you.
The day asked a lot. You answered some of it. That's fine.
Quiet is productive too. Let yourself have some.
Tomorrow's problems can wait their turn. Tonight is for slowing down.
Not everything got done. You're still okay.
Take a breath you actually feel. Then let the day go.
You made it through. That was never guaranteed, and you did it.
Be gentle with the version of you that got tired today.
